title: The Big Cheese Ultra Power Ready-Baited Rat Trap – Twinpack, Kills Rats, Ready-To-Use, Easy Disposal, Plastic, Indoor or Outdoor Use, White
features: TESTED AND PROVEN: Britain’s best-selling rat trap, with money-back guarantee included on-pack
POWERFUL: Performance tested to ensure effective control of rats – just like the traps that professionals use
READY-TO-USE: Pre-baited with highly palatable bait that is irresistible to rats
ONE-TOUCH SETTING: Just one touch is needed to set the rat traps, Captured rats are easily disposed of with a simple squeeze of the trap
REFILLABLE: Replenish bait using The Big Cheese Mouse and Rat Attractant (STV163, sold separately)
INDOOR OR OUTDOOR USE: Suitable for use in the home or outdoors in garages, garden sheds, and outbuildings
description: From the manufacturer GUARANTEED EFFECTIVE RODENT CONTROL Bait & Kill: Our Ultra Power range offers the only true single-feed rodent poison available on the amateur market. The highly palatable formula contains a powerful active ingredient that delivers a lethal dose to rodents in just one feed. Catch & Kill: We offer a selection of versatile rodent trap solutions that are highly effective, with satisfaction guaranteed. Our powerful snap traps have been designed with durability in mind to be used again and again, whilst our innovative electronic products use smart-circuit technology to deliver a quick and humane kill. Pet-safe and child-safe options are available. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Self-setting multi-catch mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping and are ideal for large rodent infestations. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced mega-sonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Pack contains 2 ready-baited traps GUARANTEED RODENT CONTROL Our on-pack pledge is "satisfaction guaranteed" reassuring you that the high-performance Ultra Power products from The Big Cheese will deliver effective results every time! Professional-quality products you can trust. Experts in rodent control for over 25 years. Ultra Power Rat Traps from The Big Cheese Professional quality ready baited traps Ultra Power Traps from The Big Cheese are performance tested to ensure effective control of rats – just like the traps that professionals use. Ultra Power traps are also easy to set and supplied baited ready-to-use. Simply remove the bait cover and squeeze the trap to set – the traps are guaranteed to kill rats. For best results place the two traps 4-5 metres apart where rats are active. Heavy infestations may require more traps. Simple one-touch trap setting. Baited and ready to use. Trap clicks open for easy disposal. Refill and reuse with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant - purchased separately. Suitable for indoor and outdoor use. Tested and proven. Part of the ULTRA POWER range. Like the professionals use The Big Cheese Ultra Power range delivers home-use rodent control solutions that are guaranteed effective - JUST LIKE THE PROFESSIONALS USE. High performance Ultra Power traps from The Big Cheese are performance tested to ensure effective control of rats – just like the traps the professionals use. Powerful and strong ensuring an instant humane kill. Baited ready to use Ultra Power Rat Traps are baited ready to use. Simply remove the bait cover and set the trap by squeezing handle until you hear a click. Place trap at a right-angle to a wall, where rats have been seen with bait well nearest the wall. Reusable Robust and strong plastic traps that are designed for repeat use. To increase catch rate, re-bait traps with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse and Rat Attractant (purchase separately). Guaranteed effective rodent control Rodent problems No-one likes the thought of a mouse or rat in their home so spotting the signs early can help avoid larger infestations. Look out for: droppings, gnaw marks, scratches, dead or live rodents, smells, noises and unusual pet behaviour. Choose the control method from The Big Cheese that's right for you. For immediate problems and prevent return, seal gaps in walls, remove food sources and use repellent products from The Big Cheese. For more information on rodent control and identifying and preventing rodent problems in your home visit the STV Pest Control website. Diet Brown rats consume an average of 50g of food per day and, despite the majority of their nutrition coming from bird-feeders or bins, they are still picky eaters and copy their friends’ habits. Grains, such as cereal, are a typical easily accessible food source for them in homes. Rats have an incredible sense of smell and can distinguish what others have eaten by the scent of food particles on their whiskers. Whatever the nutritional value of this food, be it deadly or bursting with vitamin C, the rat will assume the food is safe because another rat has eaten it and proceed to eat it themselves. Interestingly, the smaller rats in a mischief (the collective name for a group of rats) are out-competed by larger ones and made to venture out in search of secondary food sources; these are dubbed ‘taster rats’. A taster rat must inspect new food sources, and, if it survives, begrudgingly delivers the news back to its mischief who arrive and push him out of the food source to consume themselves. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
